observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the BAC Secretariat to post the NOA in the PhilGEPs within three days from issuance as required in Section 37.1.6 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 for transparency which in turn promotes good governance.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the BAC to conduct post-qualification meticulously in the determination of whether the bidder concerned complies with and is responsive to all the requirements and conditions specified in the bidding documents to ensure the ability of the bidder to undertake the contract.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require:

the Municipal Engineer to instruct all contractors to see to it that all laborers are provided with complete and appropriate PPEs and cause for the installation of warning devices at the project site whenever necessary in order not to compromise the health and safety of the workers and the public, and henceforth indicate the actual percentage of completion of the pay items in the SWA; and

the Municipal Accountant to carefully scrutinize the supporting documents of disbursement vouchers and determine with certainty the amount due to the contractors in order not to unduly benefit the latter at the expense of the municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

review the disbursement process to address the reasons for the delay in the submission of collection and disbursement documents and other reports;

require the Municipal Accountant to submit the four (4) Disbursement Vouchers to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspensions; and

instruct the Municipal Accountant that henceforth ensure that all revenue and disbursement documents shall be submitted to the Commission on Audit not later than the 10th day of the following month to permit timely post audit of these transactions so that deficiencies, if any, could be communicated to management on time for appropriate corrective action.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the BAC Chairman and the GSO Designate to strictly comply with Items 3.1.1 and 3.2.1 of the COA Circular No. 2009-001, wherein a copy of the contracts and POs irrespective of the amount, and each and every supporting documents shall be furnished to the Auditor within five (5) working days from issuance thereof in order for the latter to perform timely review/evaluation of the same and  conduct timely inspection/validation of deliveries, thus any deficiency could be communicated to management for immediate and appropriate action thereof.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to submit the quarterly financial statements on time to maximize the usefulness of the financial information and enable Management to make timely corrective measures for any errors, deficiencies or irregularities discovered through the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

instruct the BAC to fast tract the procurement process of all the programs/projects funded out of the RA No. 7171 funds which were not yet subjected to public bidding, and


require all concerned Department Heads to facilitate the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the early enjoyment of benefits they rightfully deserve.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer intensify and closely monitor the implementation of proposed priority programs/projects/activities under the 20% DF in order to deliver on time the benefits the constituents could derive therefrom and achieve socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to:

intensify the collection of real property taxes and conduct massive tax campaign in all the barangays, and


seek the cooperation of barangay officials since they have also shares in the collection of real property taxes.

Inventory committee Responsible has conduct an inventory of unserviceable properties and prepare an Inventory and Inspection Report of unserviceable properties and only drop PPE items when disposed of.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the proposed adjusting journal entry to recognize the shares of the DPWH and OBO from collections of Building Permit Fees:

We also recommended that the Municipality create and maintain a Trust Fund Account for Building Permit Fees and other charges as required under Item 2.1 of the DILG and DPWH JMC No. 1 dated July 4, 2014 and henceforth remit the corresponding shares of the DPWH and OBO.

2.9 	We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan appropriate funds from tobacco excise taxes in accordance with the guidelines on the utilization of the shares of Local Government Units (LGUs) from the RA No. 7171 and RA No. 8240, as amended by RA No. 10351, and as further amended by RA No. 11346, in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds of the benefits they rightfully deserve.

2.10	We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer identify the list of programs and projects to be implemented that are beneficial to the tobacco farmers.

3.7	We recommended that henceforth,
3.7.1	the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to observe closer monitoring and evaluation of all ongoing infrastructure projects may not be compromised and ensure completion of the project within the specified contract duration so that benefits that could be derived therefrom are immediately realized;
3.7.2	if delays are incurred in the completion of a project, implement calibrated measures provided in GPPB Circular No. 03-2019 prior to termination in part or in whole, of the work that has not been completed and accepted under a contract; and
3.7.3	impose administrative sanctions, if warranted, in accordance with Section 69 of the IRR of RA No. 9184, now governed by Rule XXI of the IRR or RA No. 12009, against erring contractors.

4.8	We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:
4.8.1	oblige the Bidding and Awards Committee (BAC) to monitor the submission of the CHSP duly approved by the DOLE from the winning bidders before the signing of contract and issuance of Notice to Proceed to ensure the protection and welfare of workers and the general public within and around the immediate vicinity of the project site, and
4.8.2	instruct the Municipal Engineer to require all contractors to see to it that all laborers are provided with complete and appropriate PPEs, part time safety practitioners, first aiders, first aid kits, and cause for the installation of warning devices at the project site whenever necessary in order not to compromise the health and safety of the workers and the general public.

5.6	We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:
5.6.1	instruct the HRMO to submit immediately the DTRs presented in Annexes A.1 to A.12 and the Applications for Leave and Travel Orders/Pass Slips shown in Annexes B.1 to B.12 to avoid suspension of the salaries received by the concerned employees, and
5.6.2	require the Municipal Accountant that henceforth, see to it that payrolls are adequately supported with DTRs and Applications for Leave and/or Travel Order/Pass Slip, if applicable.

6.8	We recommended that Management inform the Auditor in writing on the posting of infrastructure projects billboards within ten days after the award of the contract,  update the project status portion of the billboard based on the table prescribed in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 20, 2023 and comply with the submission of monthly report of on-going and completed contracts in order to promote transparency in the implementation of infrastructure project, thus the people are well informed on matters of public concern.

7.5	We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to:
7.5.1	exercise due care in the preparation of checks to avoid cancellation thereof and wastage of government resources, and
7.5.2	submit to the Audit Team the original copy of the four cancelled checks.

8.5	We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit the revenue and disbursement documents to the Audit Team until the 10th day of the following month.

9.6	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
9.6.1	instruct the GSO Designate to put permanent marking/tagging of each unit of the 44 units solar led street lights showing the source of fund, kind of PPE, year and month it was purchased, and the series per item; such marking/tagging

9.6.2	require the Municipal Engineer to submit the specific locations where the 44 sets solar led lights were installed including the markings for each unit;

9.6.3	instruct the Municipal Engineer that henceforth, put permanent markings on every unit installed and prepare complete and verifiable list of installation sites, including barangay/location names, the number of units installed per location, and the markings/serial number per unit.

9.6.4	require the Municipal Accountant that henceforth, see to it that upon final payment of the project, item c above must be attached to the disbursement voucher.

10.12	We recommended that henceforth, the Sangguniang Bayan immediately appropriate funds from RA 7171 and 8240 for the speedy implementation of the programs and projects.

10.13	We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
10.13.1	instruct the BAC to immediately start the procurement process of all the programs/projects funded out of the RA 7171 funds which were not yet subjected to public bidding, and
10.13.1	require all concerned Department Heads to facilitate the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the early enjoyment of benefits they rightfully deserve.  In addition, we recommend immediate implementation to enable the municipal government to avail of lesser costs for these programs/projects and in order that more benefits could be derived from these funds considering the unending price increase of all commodities.

11.6	We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer intensify and closely monitor the implementation of proposed priority programs/projects/activities under the 20% DF in order to deliver on time the benefits the constituents could derive therefrom and achieve socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.

12.4	We recommended that the municipality fully implement its Solid Waste Management Plan and the pertinent provisions of Republic Act. No. 9003 in order to safeguard the public health from substantial present and potential hazardous wastes and attain a safe, clean and green environment.

13.5	We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer intensify the collection of real property taxes and conduct massive tax campaign in all the barangays.  Furthermore, seek the cooperation of barangay officials since they have also shares on the collection of real property taxes. Thus, additional income would be collected to fund other priority programs and projects of the municipality which are beneficial to its constituent.

1.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that postings in the
General Ledger – Cash in Local Treasury are vouched with the recorded
transactions in the Cash Receipts Journal to ensure an accurate and reliable balance
of the account. Further, to review the account and make the necessary correcting
entry, and provide the COA with a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher.

2.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make necessary adjusting entries
in relation to the deficiencies noted above; otherwise, submit a justification as to the
correctness of existing records, and furnish the audit team copies of the same.

3.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
a. The Municipal Accountant to prepare the Subsidiary Ledgers for all
accounts and for all funds, in order to present balances in the Financial
Statements that are verifiable and thus promote reliability on said financial
statements; and
b. The Inventory Team to conduct a physical count of PPE.

4.6 We recommended that:
 a. the Municipal Accountant consults the Municipal Engineer prior to
recognizing transactions related to infrastructure to properly determine the
account to be used in recording; and,
 b. prepare adjusting journal entries in relation to the errors mentioned.

5.6 We recommended that the Chairperson of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
determine a contractor’s qualifications for the award of contract through strict
compliance with the required documents as provided by law.
5.7 Further, we recommended that the BAC Secretary determine if the mentioned
documents are on file and initiate their submission to COA.

6.6 We recommended that the Municipal Engineer regularly monitor the
implementation of projects and document it through an Inspection Report to validly
support and facilitate accurate computation of any Liquidated Damages due to the
LGU.

7.6 We recommended that the Chairperson of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
ascertain that procurement proceedings conducted by the BAC for a particular
project is included in the approved Annual Procurement Plan to ensure that the
proper procurement activities are done and well supported with appropriate
documents in accordance with the guidelines set forth in existing laws related to
procurement.
7.7 Further, require the BAC Secretary to submit to COA the Pakyaw Labor Contract
and its supporting documents.

8.8 We recommended that the LDRRM Officer ensure that the LDRRMF utilization is
directly responsive to the purpose of the fund, taking into consideration the thematic
areas of the LDRRMF.

9.7 We recommended that:
 a. The Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer to effect the return of the CY 2024 income tax deficiency and
consequently remit the same to the BIR; and
 b. Henceforth, compute tax on compensation income taking into account all laws and
regulations in force to identify the remuneration to be included in the
computation, and to appropriately classify which among those can be deducted or
exempted from withholding of taxes.

10.11 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer pay directly to the suppliers or
service providers through issuance of check, and strictly follow the withholding of
taxes on all payment to suppliers/sellers who are subjected to 1% or 2% EWT, 5%
GVAT and 3% GPT, and remit the same to BIR on time.
10.12 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that payments for
internet and communication expenses are duly substantiated with an official receipt.

11.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant
to submit the accounts to COA immediately.

We recommended that the LCE: 

Enforce a centralized system on the procurement of supplies, materials and equipment for proper control on the acquisition and issuances thereof wherein the GSO/Municipal Treasurer will determine the items to be procured based on the Consolidated Annual Procurement Plan of the Municipality and require the BAC to conduct a public bidding for items not available in the Procurement Service (PS)-DBM;

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:  

require the Municipal Accountant to take up the proposed adjusting journal entries as shown in Item 1.7.1, page 41 of the 2023 AAR.



instruct the Municipal Engineer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the Quarterly Report on Government Projects/Programs/Activities as basis of the latter in recording completed infrastructure projects in the books of accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:  

instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to see to it that items of expenditures in the budget are classified correctly as Personal Services, Miscellaneous and Other Operating Expenses and Capital Outlay;


require the Municipal Accountant to consider the purpose of the expenditure in recording transactions in the books of accounts; and


instruct the Municipal Accountant take up the correcting entry as shown in Item 2.3, page 43 of the 2023 AAR.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the:

Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Accounting Office a schedule of unreleased checks at the end of every month or quarter, and



Municipal Accountant to restore the cash equivalent of all unreleased checks by debiting Cash in Bank – LCCA and crediting the appropriate payable/liability accounts supported with a working paper at the end of the month or quarter and by preparing a JEV at the end of the year based on the submitted schedule from the Municipal Treasurer.  In addition, in the ensuing year, prepare a JEV to reverse the entries made covering the restoration in the previous year.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher as shown in Item 4.5.1, page 46 of the 2023 AAR. 



instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant that henceforth, reconcile their records on RPT and SET Receivables regularly to reflect the correct balances of these accounts together with the Deferred RPT and SET in the financial statements, and


require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the balances of RPT and SET Receivables are duly supported with schedules as required under Section 58 of PD 1445.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

require the GSO-Designate to prepare the IIRUP and file an application with the Commission on Audit for the disposal of the unserviceable properties, and

instruct the Municipal Accountant that upon disposal, prepare the necessary accounting entries to derecognize these PPEs from the books of accounts in order to present fairly the balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements.


require Management to be guided with the provisions of COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2024-001 dated January 30, 2024 on the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties.

To correct the e aforementioned deficiencies, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

require the Municipal Accountant to prepare correcting entry as shown in Item 6.8.1, page 50 of the 2023 AAR.



instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant that henceforth, observe the capitalization threshold of items to be classified as PPE which is ₱50,000.00,   in the preparation of the budget and the recording of financial transactions in the books of accounts, respectively.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

require the Municipal Accountant to validate the claimants  of the unpaid obligations in the amount of ₱2,305,921.98 which remained outstanding for more than two years and once claimants and existence of liability could no longer be verified, a report thereon shall be provided to the Municipal Mayor that will be presented to the Sangguniang Bayan which will in turn be the basis of the latter to pass a resolution recommending the dropping of these obligations from the books  to fairly present the balance of this account in the financial statements; and

instruct the Municipal Accountant to only recognize accounts payable based on valid claims and are properly substantiated with sufficient evidence.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Accountant that henceforth be guided by the IPSAS Chart of Accounts in recording financial transactions to reflect the correct balances of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the GSO Designate to notify the Audit Team of all deliveries of procured goods within twenty-four (24) hours from the acceptance so that the Auditor could conduct timely inspection/validation of same and communicate the results thereof for Management’s consideration.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require:

the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the cash allocated for Trust Liability – DRRMF from the bank account of General Fund to the bank account of Trust Fund to facilitate accounting and monitoring of said fund and avoid confusion on the usage of check booklet to be used; and

the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary accounting entries after the bank transfer to reflect the correct balances of Cash in Bank for each fund and minimize the balances of the Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit collections intact with the authorized depository bank whenever  the latter goes to the bank for the encashment of check/s  or when collections reach Ᵽ10,000.00 to avoid the accumulation of huge cash  on hand, thereby minimizing the risk of possible loss, misuse or misappropriation of government funds and at the same time enable the municipality to earn additional income in the form of interest on bank deposits.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:

submit justification/ explanation on the aforementioned lapses;


require all the collectors that at the close of each business day, they accomplish all the data in the RCD, affix their signatures therein and turn over all their collections to the Municipal Treasurer, and

verify the correctness of all entries in the RCD prepared by every collector before receiving the collections and affixing her signature therein.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

require the Municipal Accountant not to process additional cash advance of any official or employee unless the previous cash advance granted is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made, and

instruct all officials and employees to submit liquidation documents and refund excess cash advance if any, when the purpose of the cash advance has been served and to liquidate all cash advances at the end of the year.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the 39 cancelled checks; and



instruct the Municipal Treasurer that henceforth, attach all the cancelled checks to the Report of Checks Issued and submit the same together with the cancelled checks to the Municipal Accountant who shall submit these documents to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

require the person-in-charge in the issuance of Driver’s Trip Tickets to put serial number in chronological order on all Driver’s Trip Tickets and see to it that the same are approved by the authorized official before these are issued to the drivers;

instruct all drivers to fill out the corresponding data required in the Driver’s Trip Tickets before and after the trip, and

require the GSO Designate and the Municipal Accountant to assist the drivers in the preparation of the Monthly Report of Official Travels    and instruct them to prepare the Report of Fuel Consumption for all vehicles/heavy equipment owned by the municipality and submit the same to the Auditor within the first ten days of the succeeding month.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, furnish the same to the LDRRMO for the latter to reconcile with his record and submit to the COA Auditor not later than the 15th day after the end of each month so that errors/deficiencies could be communicated to Management on time for appropriate corrective action.

We recommended and Management agreed that, the “Requesting Official” portion of the CAFOA and Box A of the disbursement voucher of Special Education Fund be signed by the district supervisor or his/her authorized representative to enable the said official to monitor disbursements and accomplishments and most importantly, to institute corrective/remedial measures to ensure full attainment of targets.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
1.7.1	require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following adjusting journal entries in order to present fairly the balances of the affected accounts:
To reclassify the completed projects to their appropriate PPE accounts.
To correct the double entry made for the Construction of Flood Control in Brgy. Atabay.
1.7.2	instruct the Municipal Engineer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the Quarterly Report on Government Projects/Programs/Activities as basis of the latter in recording completed infrastructure projects in the books of accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
2.4.1	instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to see to it that items of expenditures in the budget are classified correctly as Personal Services, Miscellaneous and Other Operating Expenses and Capital Outlay;
2.4.2	require the Municipal Accountant to consider the purpose of the expenditure in recording transactions in the books of accounts; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant take up the correcting entry as follows:

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the:
3.6.1	Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Accounting Office a schedule of unreleased checks at the end of every month or quarter, and
3.6.2	Municipal Accountant to restore the cash equivalent of all unreleased checks by debiting Cash in Bank – LCCA and crediting the appropriate payable/liability accounts supported with a working paper at the end of the month or quarter and by preparing a JEV at the end of the year based on the submitted schedule from the Municipal Treasurer.  In addition, in the ensuing year, prepare a JEV to reverse the entries made covering the restoration in the previous year.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
4.5.1	require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to correct the balances of the affected accounts as follows:
instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant that henceforth, reconcile their records on RPT and SET Receivables regularly to reflect the correct balances of these accounts together with the Deferred RPT and SET in the financial statements, and
4.5.3	require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the balances of RPT and SET Receivables are duly supported with schedules as required under Section 58 of PD 1445.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
5.6.1	require the GSO-Designate to prepare the IIRUP and file an application with the Commission on Audit for the disposal of the unserviceable properties, and
5.6.2	instruct the Municipal Accountant that upon disposal, prepare the necessary accounting entries to derecognize these PPEs from the books of accounts in order to present fairly the balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
5.6.3	Require Management to be guided with the provisions of COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2024-001 dated January 30, 2024 on the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties.

6.8.1	require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following correcting entry:
instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant that henceforth, observe the capitalization threshold of items to be classified as PPE which is ₱50,000.00,   in the preparation of the budget and the recording of financial transactions in the books of accounts, respectively.

7.8.1	require the Municipal Accountant to validate the claimants  of the unpaid obligations in the amount of ₱2,305,921.98 which remained outstanding for more than two years and once claimants and existence of liability could no longer be verified, a report thereon shall be provided to the Municipal Mayor that will be presented to the Sangguniang Bayan which will in turn be the basis of the latter to pass a resolution recommending the dropping of these obligations from the books  to fairly present the balance of this account in the financial statements; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to only recognize accounts payable based on valid claims and are properly substantiated with sufficient evidence.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Accountant that henceforth be guided by the IPSAS Chart of Accounts in recording financial transactions to reflect the correct balances of accounts in the financial statements.

11.6	We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the GSO Designate to notify the Audit Team of all deliveries of procured goods within twenty-four (24) hours from the acceptance so that the Auditor could conduct timely inspection/validation of same and communicate the results thereof for Management’s consideration.

12.3.4	We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the BAC Secretariat to post the NOA in the PhilGEPs within three days from issuance as required in Section 37.1.6 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 for transparency which in turn promotes good governance.
12.4.4	We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the BAC to conduct post-qualification meticulously in the determination of whether the bidder concerned complies with and is responsive to all the requirements and conditions specified in the bidding documents to ensure the ability of the bidder to undertake the contract.

13.5.1	the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the cash allocated for Trust Liability – DRRMF from the bank account of General Fund to the bank account of Trust Fund to facilitate accounting and monitoring of said fund and avoid confusion on the usage of check booklet to be used; and
13.5.2 the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary accounting entries after the bank transfer to reflect the correct balances of Cash in Bank for each fund and minimize the balances of the Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds accounts.

14.6.1	the Municipal Engineer to instruct all contractors to see to it that all laborers are provided with complete and appropriate PPEs and cause for the installation of warning devices at the project site whenever necessary in order not to compromise the health and safety of the workers and the public, and henceforth indicate the actual percentage of completion of the pay items in the SWA; and
14.6.2	the Municipal Accountant to carefully scrutinize the supporting documents of disbursement vouchers and determine with certainty the amount due to the contractors in order not to unduly benefit the latter at the expense of the municipality.

15.8	We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit collections intact with the authorized depository bank whenever  the latter goes to the bank for the encashment of check/s  or when collections reach Ᵽ10,000.00 to avoid the accumulation of huge cash  on hand, thereby minimizing the risk of possible loss, misuse or misappropriation of government funds and at the same time enable the municipality to earn additional income in the form of interest on bank deposits.

18.5.1	require the Municipal Accountant not to process additional cash advance of any official or employee unless the previous cash advance granted is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made, and
instruct all officials and employees to submit liquidation documents and refund excess cash advance if any, when the purpose of the cash advance has been served and to liquidate all cash advances at the end of the year.

19.5.1 require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the 39 cancelled checks; and
instruct the Municipal Treasurer that henceforth, attach all the cancelled checks to the Report of Checks Issued and submit the same together with the cancelled checks to the Municipal Accountant who shall submit these documents to the Office of the Auditor.

21.6	We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the BAC Chairman and the GSO Designate to strictly comply with Items 3.1.1 and 3.2.1 of the COA Circular No. 2009-001, wherein a copy of the contracts and POs irrespective of the amount, and each and every supporting documents shall be furnished to the Auditor within five (5) working days from issuance thereof in order for the latter to perform timely review/evaluation of the same and  conduct timely inspection/validation of deliveries, thus any deficiency could be communicated to management for immediate and appropriate action thereof.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to submit the quarterly financial statements on time to maximize the usefulness of the financial information and enable Management to make timely corrective measures for any errors, deficiencies or irregularities discovered through the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, furnish the same to the LDRRMO for the latter to reconcile with his record and submit to the COA Auditor not later than the 15th day after the end of each month so that errors/deficiencies could be communicated to Management on time for appropriate corrective action.

24.6	We recommended and Management agreed that, the “Requesting Official” portion of the CAFOA and Box A of the disbursement voucher of Special Education Fund be signed by the district supervisor or his/her authorized representative to enable the said official to monitor disbursements and accomplishments and most importantly, to institute corrective/remedial measures to ensure full attainment of targets.

25.11 	We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
25.11.1 	instruct the BAC to fast tract the procurement process of all the programs/projects funded out of the RA No. 7171 funds which were not yet subjected to public bidding, and
require all concerned Department Heads to facilitate the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the early enjoyment of benefits they rightfully deserve.

26.6	We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer intensify and closely monitor the implementation of proposed priority programs/projects/activities under the 20% DF in order to deliver on time the benefits the constituents could derive therefrom and achieve socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.

27.4	We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to:
27.4.1	intensify the collection of real property taxes and conduct massive tax campaign in all the barangays, and
27.4.2	seek the cooperation of barangay officials since they have also shares in the collection of real property taxes.

1.4 We recommended that:
a) For unclaimed checks, the Municipal Treasurer should send a written notice
to the payees one month before the checks become stale. Stale checks shall be
reported and attached to the RCI prepared during the period of cancellation.
b) The Municipal Accountant should make the necessary adjusting entries of the
stale checks and furnish the audit team copies of the same.

2.8 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a) Carefully scrutinize the account numbers written in the deposit slip before
depositing to the bank to ensure that the correct account where the funds are
supposed to be deposited will be credited pursuant to Section 94, Volume 1 of
NGAs Manual for LGUs;
b) Regularly request a copy of the bank statements or snapshots to check whether
the deposits, disbursements and other transactions reflected therein pertain to
the LGU; and
c) Effect the transfer of the subject funds to the Trust Fund bank account.

3.7 We recommended that the Inventory committee conduct an inventory of
unserviceable properties and prepare an Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property based on the inspection.
3.8 Further, we recommended that the Municipal Account reverse the derecognition of
the subject PPE items, and only drop PPE items when disposed of.

4.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) prepare a list of the completed projects in the barangays and submit it to the
Office of the Local Chief Executive and the Sangguniang Bayan for
appropriate decision;
b) Make necessary correcting entries on the reclassification made, if warranted,
based on the decision of the LCE and SB;
c) Furnish the Audit Team a copy of the correcting entries, or the resolution of
the SB in connection with letter “a.”, together with the related documents on
transfer, whichever is applicable.
d) Continuously prepare PPE Ledger Cards for completeness of the information
and furnish the audit team with the same.

5.12 We recommended that:
a) The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer reconcile their
respective records/registries with the CY 2022 balance per audit, to accurately
determine the amount of unexpended LDRRM Fund. Henceforth, reconcile
both records at the end of the year.
b) The Municipal Accountant to make the necessary accounting entry to record
the transfer of the unexpended balance to the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF.

6.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer
maintain separate records or registries for the 20% Development Fund to facilitate
monitoring and reconciliation of balances and prevent any errors in the balance
presented in the Statement of Comparison Budgeted and Actual Amounts concerning
the said fund.
6.7 Further, as to the herein-noted variance, the Municipal Budget Officer to submit a
reconciliation or a detailed computation of the balances arrived in the SAOB and
submit the same to the auditor.

observations.recommendation:
A. The Post-Qualification Evaluation Report did not contain the verification, validation and ascertaining all the statements made and documents submitted by the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid (LCB) on the following criteria hereunder enumerated, manifesting the non-conduct of proper evaluation whether said bidder has passed all the requirements and conditions as specified in the bidding documents, hence his capacity to undertake the contract is doubtful.

                     We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to meticulously conduct post-qualification to determine whether the bidder concerned complies with and is responsive to all the requirements and conditions as specified in the bidding documents, hence the ability of the bidder to undertake the contract is ensured.

B. The Notice of Award (NOA) on the procurement of 54 infrastructure projects and 13 for goods costing ₱126,144,312.93 and ₱29,183,954.00 respectively were posted in the PhilGEPS website beyond the prescribed period required in Sections 37.1.6 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184, thus the objectives of the procurement law to promote transparency and efficiency in the conduct of procurement in the government was not served. 

 	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretariat to post the NOA in the PhilGEPs within three days from issuance as required in Section 37.1.6 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184, in order to promote transparency and efficiency in the conduct of procurement in the government.

C.   The required minimum major equipment was not specified in the Bid Data Sheet (BDS) of the 18 contracts worth ₱7,717,917.54, hence important information required in the contract was not conveyed to prospective bidders, thus may mislead interested bidders and a risk that they may not meet the requirements of the contract. Likewise, there were no listed equipment in the report of List of Equipment, Owned or Leased Pledged to the Proposed Contract, manifesting the lack of proper verification of documents and conduct of post-qualification, thus the capacity of the contractor to undertake the contract was not ascertained.

          We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to verify that the bidding documents are completely filled out showing all the important provisions required in a particular procurement activity in order to convey accurate information to prospective bidders. Likewise, to conduct proper post-qualification and verification of the submitted eligibility documents in order to ascertain the capacity of the bidder to undertake the contract.

D.   Three procurements for infrastructure and two procurements for goods totaling ₱2,161,169.05 were not included in the approved Annual Procurement Plan (APP) of the Municipality for CY 2022 contrary to Section 7 of the RIRR of RA 9184, hence casting doubt on the validity and legality of these transactions.

 	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to immediately submit the Supplemental Procurement Plan on the abovementioned disbursements to ascertain the validity and legality of these transactions, henceforth, ensure that a supplemental Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) is included in the amended APP duly approved by the Head of the Procuring entity (HOPE).

E.    Management entered into contract despite the inability of the contractors to secure approval of the Construction Safety and Health Program (CSHP) from the Department of Labor and Employment DOLE on the 52 infrastructure projects costing ₱49,199,874.92 contrary to Sections 37.2.2 and 7.2.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184, hence the protection and welfare of workers employed and the general public within and around the construction worksite were not ensured.

          We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC strict compliance with the provisions set forth in Sections 37.2.2 and 37.2.3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 by requiring the submission of CSHP duly approved by the DOLE prior to the approval of the contract, to ensure the safety and protection of the workers employed as well as the general public. 

           Likewise, Management should refrain from entering into contract when requisite documents are not present to ensure validity of the contract and to guarantee security during the project implementation.

that the:
a) Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer regularly
reconcile their respective records
to ensure correctness of the
account balance presented in the
financial statements;
b) Municipal Accountant:
1. prepares and submits necessary
adjusting entries for errors tabulated
in Table 4 above and reclassifies the
Trust Fund, Cash – Local Treasury on
the adjusted collections of the
previous MT to Other Receivables
account; and
c) The Local Chief Executive issues a demand
letter to the previous Municipal
Treasurer for the full restitution of the
undeposited collections
amounting to P1,045,570.79, or
files an appropriate legal action to
recover the full amount.
that the Municipal Accountant ensures that entries made to the account are supported with appropriate records. Likewise, review errors and make the necessary accounting entry.

that:
a) The Municipal Accountant:
1. Make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the noted errors; and
2. Hereafter, review the reports and the supporting documents submitted by the Municipal Treasurer to properly recognize and reflect the correct revenue in the financial statements.

that the Local Chief Executive as the approving officer of all grants of cash advances must require the Municipal Accountant to have an updated monitoring of all cash advances and strictly implement the non-granting of the additional cash advances to officers or employees with outstanding balances. Moreover, for the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to all officers and employees with outstanding balance as of December 31, 2022, copy furnished the Commission on Audit in order to facilitate the latter in taking appropriate action for that matter.

that the Municipal Treasurer:
a) Prohibits use of collections for petty expenses and requires the daily remittance of collections; and
b) Set up a petty cash fund to address the petty expenses of the agency and ensure the strict implementation of the established control for petty cash funds under the imprest system to avoid loss and misuse of government resources.

that the:
a) The Municipal Accountant computes the withholding tax on the OCA payments to the health care workers to which said tax is due and initiates the remittance of the same to the Bureau of Internal Revenue.
b) The Health Care Workers concerned refund the amount as computed by the Municipal Accountant representing withholding tax on the OCA they received.
that the Bids and Awards Committee strictly follow the reglementary period on the submission of contracts to ensure timely review and compliance with any deficiencies that may be noted.

that the Local Chief Executive shall enjoin:
a) The Human Resources Management Officer to carefully study and plan the requirements of the LGU so that proper positioning of staff in the LGU will be attained and to avoid hiring of unnecessary personnel; and
b) The Municipal Treasurer to stop processing of payroll without the supporting documentary requirements evidencing the validity, propriety and legality of the payment of wages to the JO employees.
that the Local Chief Executive instruct the officers concerned to stop using cash advances other than the purpose it was granted and to return the same if unused.

that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to present those excess funds under the 20% development fund in order for it to be utilized on identified priority development projects; and
b. As the Head of the Local Development Council, to ensure that projects which are identified to be financed from the 20% development fund are well-planned and feasible so as to optimize the use of the fund.

that the Local Chief Executive shall:
a. Reconcile their records to arrive at a corrected RAAO, Report on the Utilization of the LDRRMF and disclosure on the Notes to Financial Statements, and submit the same to COA; and
b. Regularly reconcile their records on the LDRRMF to ensure correctness of reports in the future.

that management require the MDRRMO to regularly submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC to this office, and further require the accounting and budget office units to assist in the preparation of the said report to be certified by the Municipal Accountant.

that the Local Chief Executive shall:
a. Direct the Barangay Chairperson and the Barangay Treasurer to immediately liquidate after completion of the purpose of the financial assistance from the LGU and other government agencies and in the event of change/resignation of the latter, to effect proper turnover of documents to his/her successor; and

We recommended that the LCE:
Require the Municipal Accountant and concerned officials strict compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012;
Instruct the concerned officials to submit the lacking documents to the Municipal Accountant who will verify the completeness and accuracy thereof and subsequently submit the same to the Auditor in order for the latter to verify the validity, propriety and correctness of these documents and compliance of said transactions with the abovementioned provisions of law and regulations; and
Moving forward, support all disbursement vouchers with complete and accurate documents before effecting payment thereof to ascertain the validity, propriety and correctness of the transactions.

We recommended that the LCE require the Property Officer to notify the Audit Team of all deliveries of procured goods within twenty-four (24) hours from the acceptance so that the Auditor could conduct timely inspection/validation of same and communicate the results thereof for Management’s consideration.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Bids and Awards Committee and the Property Officer strict compliance with Items 3.1.1 and 3.2.1 of the COA Circular No. 2009-001, wherein a copy of the contracts and POs irrespective of the amount, and each and every supporting documents shall be furnished to the Auditor within five (5) working days from the issuance thereof in order for the latter to perform timely review/evaluation of the same and therefore could conduct timely inspection/validation of deliveries, thus any deficiency could be communicated to management for immediate and appropriate action thereof.

We recommended that the LCE shall appoint/designate an inspectorate committee or an authorized agency inspector who shall inspect all deliveries of supplies and materials to conform with the provisions set forth in the Training Handbook on PSMS and Section 18, Chapter 7 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, hence proper management and sound internal control in the procurement, delivery, inspection and acceptance of supplies and materials of the agency could be ensured.

We recommended that the LCE shall appoint/designate an inspectorate committee or an authorized agency inspector who shall conduct the pre inspection of destroyed/damaged properties for repair and post inspection after the repair of said properties to conform with the provisions set forth in Chapter 4 of the Training Handbook on Property and Supply Management System, hence  the principle of sound internal control and check and balance in the management of repair and maintenance of agency properties is observed.

We recommended that the LCE require:
Concerned Heads of the Requesting Unit to completely fill out the CAFOA showing all the details pertaining to the function, allotment class and expense code, thus the Municipal Budget Officer could facilitate recording, review and monitoring of the charging of obligations to their respective registries; and
The Municipal Accountant being responsible in the processing of the CAFOA to examine that the same is properly and completely accomplished before a particular transaction is processed.

We recommended that the LCE:
ensure that identified programs, projects and activities under the 20% DF are implemented as planned to fully attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the municipality;
require the concerned officials to review the feasibility of the unimplemented programs/projects and plan for the necessary realignment of these funds to a more viable priority development projects; and
encourage the LGU to formulate plans that are attainable within a year to maximize the benefits that could be derived from the full utilization of the fund.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to ensure the optimal utilization of the RA 7171 and 8240 Municipal Share through proper and timely implementation of the identified PPAs so that the benefits expected from its timely completion will be delivered to the tobacco farmers.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the LDRRMO to:
ensure the effective, efficient and optimal implementation of all the PPAs identified in the LDRRMF Investment Plan to support the achievement of the goals and objectives in reducing/mitigating disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the LGU;
review the feasibility of the unimplemented PPAs and plan for the necessary realignment to identified more viable programs that harmonize with the present needs/demands of the constituents; and
formulate plans/programs that are attainable within a year to maximize the benefits that could be derived from the full utilization/implementation of the fund.

1.6 We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to
disclose appropriately in the notes to financial statements any reconciliation
resulting from the foregoing events.

2.15 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Trace the difference in the financial reports and make the necessary
correcting entry so as to reflect the correct balance and render reliable
report for decision-making purposes by its intended users; and
b. Prepare & submit the lacking monthly BRS of the Special Trust Fund
Accounts for CY 2021 and thereafter in accordance with the
requirements set-forth in Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA
Circular No. 96-011.

a. Instruct the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to
prepare and maintain the property records as mandated by existing
rules and regulations in order that all items reported in the PPE
account are properly supported with records.
b. Initiate the compliance of the guidelines and procedures set forth in
COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of the PPE
account so that the PPE balance presented in the Financial Statements
is verifiable as to completeness and existence.

4.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to carefully scrutinize each transaction in order to properly
recognize the same in the books of accounts and thus prevent any
overstatement or understatement in the financial statements. Moreover, for
the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect
unrecorded asset and depreciation.

5.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Instruct the General Services Officer and all officers concerned to adhere
to the guidelines on property and supply management laid out in the
Handbook on Property and Supply Management and in the NGAS for
LGUs in order to facilitate proper accounting recognition of inventories.
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the criteria on the
recognition of inventory and its subsequent recognition as expense in order
to prevent any overstatement or understatement in the financial statement.

7.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to carefully analyze each transaction and adhere to the recognition
criteria established for each account in order to fairly present the effects of
these transactions on the financial statements. Furthermore, for the Municipal
Accountant to make the necessary correcting entries for transactions where
asset should be recognized instead of expense.

8.11 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) through its Chairperson to ensure compliance with
all the provisions in the procurement law and related rules and regulations in
the conduct of the procurement process to ensure completeness of
documentary evidence and thus promote transparency and competitiveness in
all government procurement. Likewise, to instruct the BAC Secretariat, to
submit the lacking documents to prevent issuance of notice of suspension of
transactions on contract payments.

9.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to adhere to the documentary requirements provided in the COA
Circular and other regulations before signifying its approval for payment in
the Disbursement Voucher in order that transactions are well supported to
prove its validity and propriety and to prevent the issuance of Notice of
Suspension if evidence warrants. Furthermore, for the Municipal Accountant
to submit the lacking documents herein noted.

10.8. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MEO to
regularly monitor the status of completion of infrastructure projects and make
a demand if warranted to prevent delay which hampers the attainment of
desired community development. Likewise, to prepare an Inspection Report
especially for projects that are still on-going as of the expected date of
completion, and to compute for Liquidated Damages to support deduction to
any payments due to the contractor. Moreover, as to the above findings, for
the Municipal Engineer to compute for the liquidated damages and direct the
Municipal Accountant to deduct it from any claims that the contractor still
has against the LGU.

a. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to present to the Local Finance
Committee those excess funds under the 20% development fund in order
for it to be utilized on identified priority development projects; and
b. As the Head of the Local Development Council, to ensure that projects
which are identified to be financed from the 20% development fund are
well planned and feasible so as to optimize the use of the fund.

a. Reconcile their records to arrive at a corrected RAAO, Report on the
Utilization of the LDRRMF and disclosure on the Notes to Financial
Statements, and submit the same to COA; and,

of reports in the future.

14.8. We recommended that management require the MDRRMO to regularly
submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th
day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC to this office, and
further require the accounting and budget office units to assist in the
preparation of the said report to be certified by the Municipal Accountant.

15.15. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall:
• Direct the Barangay Chairperson and the Barangay Treasurer to
immediately liquidate after completion of the purpose of the financial
assistance from the LGU and other government agencies and in the
event of change/resignation of the latter, to effect proper turnover of
documents to his/her successor; and
• Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor and strictly
implement the rules underlying all financial assistance granted to the
barangay and to make necessary follow-up of its liquidation as
provided for in COA Circular No. 94-013 and the stipulation in the
MOA.

1.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1.7.1 Instruct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Budget
Officer with a copy of the SWA of the above-mentioned on-going and
completed projects as basis for the recording of obligations; and
1.7.2 Instruct the Municipal Accountant to cause the preparation of
necessary journal entry/entries to correct the above-computed
understatement of the CIP-Infrastructure Assets, Road Networks
and Accounts Payable in the amount of P8,154,627.77, P299,004.83
and P8,453,632.60, respectively, in order to present fairly the
affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Examine prior year transactions to locate the differences found in audit; b. Draw journal entry vouchers to record all reconciling items that require adjustments and correction in the general as well as subsidiary ledgers; and c. strictly observe the regular and timely submission of BRS.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant the following actions in order to establish proper classification and valuation of the accounts: a. Draw necessary journal entries to reflect the correct balance of CIP account; b. Strictly observe the regular and timely transfer of completed projects to its appropriate PPE account; and, c. Duly examine the propriety of journal entries before recording the same to the affected accounts.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the standards on financial statements’ presentation and appropriate disclosure to achieve reliability of the accounts to afford users of the financial data with relevant informative values.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive oblige the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the required details in the Notes to Financial Statements on the unexpended LDRRMF to substantiate the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and provide confidence to the users of Financial Statements over transparency and accountability of its information to users or stakeholders of the financial statements.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and other local officials ensure proper application and implementation of the established funds to address future similar crisis to ascertain regularity of transactions and afford full benefits to the affected constituents.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of documents on fund utilization and liquidation in future similar operations to ascertain that government resources are properly applied and appropriately utilized.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall ensure exclusive provision of fuel to properly marked government vehicles to achieve regularity of transactions through proper utilization of government funds and properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Mayor:
Require all Department Heads strict compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. The Municipal Accountant to meticulously scrutinize all disbursement vouchers submitted for processing and ensure that these are completely supported with the required documents before effecting payment thereof to ascertain the validity, legality, propriety and correctness of the financial transactions.
As regards the foregoing transactions, instruct the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents and address the deficiencies noted to determine the validity, propriety and correctness of all the transactions and in compliance with the abovementioned provisions of law and regulations.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to verify and go over his computations on the foregoing liquidated damages and submit immediately the corresponding WSO/WRO and supporting documents or valid proofs of unworkable conditions to validate the reasonableness of suspensions and accuracy of reported liquidated damages.
Moving forward, adapt the calibrated actions in response to delays in the implementation of infrastructure projects provided in Item 7 and 8 of Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 and Item 4.2 of GPPB Circular No. 03-2019 to ensure that infrastructure projects are fully accomplished in accordance with the contract duration.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to strictly comply with the provisions set forth in COA Circular No. 2013-004. Accomplish and update signboards/billboards to enable the public to see and read relevant information for them to assess if PPAs are fairly implemented and justifiably completed which in turn promotes good governance through transparency.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor:
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to strictly observe implementation of the CSHP and integrate the same to the overall project cost under Pay Item "SPL- Construction Safety and Health" as a lump sum amount and quantify in the detailed estimate pursuant to Section III.A of the DPWH DO No. 56, s. 2005; in order to reflect/include the same to the total recorded cost of the infrastructure project; and
Require the contractors to secure approval from DOLE for their CSHP and observe strict compliance and implementation of the safety programs to ensure the protection and welfare of workers employed as well as the safety of the general public within the vicinity of the construction site during the contract implementation.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer:
intensify on-site supervision and evaluation of performance on the abovementioned projects to resolve any problems and bottlenecks;
submit approved WSO/WRO and supporting documents or valid proofs of unworkable conditions to validate the reasonableness of the delay; and
adapt the calibrated actions in response to delays in the implementation of infrastructure projects provided in Item 4.2 of GPPB Circular No. 03-2019 and Item 7 and 8 of Annex E of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184 to ensure completion of succeeding projects in accordance with the contract duration.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Instruct the BAC Secretariat as the main support unit of the BAC to prioritize their functions to:
submit the APP to the GPPB on or before the end of January of the budget year, and shall be posted in accordance with E.O. 662, series of 2007, as amended;
post/advertise the approved bidding documents in the PhilGEPS website from the time that the Invitation to Bid/Request for Expression of Interest is first advertised/posted until the deadline for the submission and receipt of bids pursuant to Section 17.3 of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184;
post the NOA and NTP in the PhilGEPS within the prescribed period pursuant to Sections 37.1.6. and 37.4.2.; and
notify in writing all bidders who submitted their bid proposals in harmony with Section 25.7 of the 2016 IRR.
Instruct the BAC to require the bidders to submit SLCC as part of the technical documents, supported with a Certificate of Final Acceptance from the procuring entity to ensure the capability and competence to undertake contracts.
Instruct the BAC to ensure that the list of contractor’s major equipment units pledged qualify with the minimum requirement specified in the BDS to determine the ability of the bidder to carry out the contract.
Instruct the BAC to meticulously conduct the process of post-qualification to ensure that the awarded bidder qualifies all the requirements and conditions specified in the Bidding Documents and to guarantee their capability and competence to undertake government projects.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor require the LDRRMO prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the prescribed format under COA Circular No. 2012-002 to show extensively the pertinent information in the utilization of DRRM fund.
Likewise, prepare the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format and submit the same on or before the 15th day after the end of each   month to the COA Auditor in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to enable the timely evaluation of the utilization of the fund.

1.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Examine prior year cash in bank transactions to locate the differences
found in audit;
b. Draw journal entry vouchers to record all reconciling items that require
adjustments and correction in the general as well as subsidiary ledgers;
and
c. strictly observe the regular and timely submission of BRS.

2.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant the following actions in order to establish proper classification and
valuation of the accounts:
a. Draw necessary journal entries to reflect the correct balance of CIP
account;
b. Strictly observe the regular and timely transfer of completed projects to its
appropriate PPE account; and,
c. Duly examine the propriety of journal entries before recording the same to
the affected accounts.

3.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal
Accountant to strictly comply with the standards on financial statements’
presentation and appropriate disclosure to achieve reliability of the accounts
to afford users of the financial data with relevant informative values.

4.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to effect necessary adjustments on their
respective books to establish appropriate balance for the Trust Liabilities-
DRRMF account, thereby achieve fair valuation and presentation of the
financial statements.

5.4 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive oblige the Municipal
Accountant to strictly observe the required details in the Notes to Financial
Statements on the unexpended LDRRMF to substantiate the Trust Liabilities-
DRRMF account and provide confidence to the users of Financial Statements
over transparency and accountability of its utilization.

6.11 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and other local officials
ensure proper application and implementation of the funds established to
address future similar crisis to ascertain regularity of transactions and afford
full benefits to the affected constituents.

7.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to ensure the completeness of documents on fund utilization and
liquidation in future similar operations to ascertain that government resources
are properly applied for and appropriately utilized.

8.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall ensure exclusive
provision of fuel to properly marked government vehicles to achieve regularity
of the transactions through proper utilization of government funds and
properties.

We have recommended that Management provide an adequate budget for solid waste management to ensure effectiveness and sustainability of its Solid Waste Management programs/projects. Barangays should be required to create Barangay SWM Committee and prepare and implement solid

 waste management policies.

We have also recommended that partnership and linkages with schools and private sector be established to assist in the implementation of SWM programs and to intensify their IEC campaign pursuant to Section 55 of RA 9003.  

Also, the following were recommended:
1. Separation of collection for biodegradable and non-biodegradable waste pursuant to Section 21 of RA 9003.
2.	Rehabilitation of the Municipality’s closed dumpsite and its conversion controlled dumpsite and thereafter into a sanitary landfill.
3.	Organization of trainings for recycling activities and composting technologies and its adoption.
4.	Integration of SWM orientation upon renewal/application of Mayor’s Permit and business licenses as part of its strategies to ensure an effective solid waste management program in the entire municipality.

We recommend that management observe the rules and regulations on the authorized deductions from the salaries of employees as provided in the General Appropriations Act issued every year before entering into Memorandum of Agreement to avail loans for its employees from private lending institutions. Let the individual employees remit their loan payments to these lending institutions.
observations.reason: Lack of intervention on the part of the LCE to compel the concerned officials to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit all the aforementioned reports in compliance with Section 6.05 of the COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 347 of RA 7160 regularly and on time.

Section 7.1 of the COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 specifically provides that the head of the agency who is primarily responsible for all funds and property pertaining to his agency shall ensure that the required financial and other reports and statements are submitted by the concerned agency officials in such form and within the period prescribed by the Commission.
observations.reason: Lack of Intervention on the part of the LCE to compel the concerned officials to comply the submission of accounts and financial reports within the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management conduct an annual physical inventory of all its properties, provide all the necessary data in the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and the result of the physical inventory of the PPE shall reconcile with the balance in the property cards and any discrepancies should be immediately verified and adjusted.

We recommended that the management dispose immediately those assets considered unserviceable to avoid further devaluation of the properties and strictly follow the process of disposal as prescribed in the COA Circular 92-386 “Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management”. Furthermore, the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) shall be used as a basis to record dropping from the books the unserviceable properties carried in the Property, Plant and Equipment and Inventory accounts.

We recommended that management prioritize the processing and transferring of the title of the land in the name of the Municipality to document the legality of ownership of the land.  Furthermore, provide the necessary data in the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to give brief description of the property and show proof of ownership. In addition to the deed of absolute sale executed by the property owner and the government, the Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT), Tax Declaration (TD) and Real Property Tax Certificate must be in the name of the procuring entity or annotation of the Deed of Absolute Sale to show proof of ownership over the property or land acquired and make them available anytime for immediate reference in the validation of the recorded Land in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Government and the Municipal School Board adhere to the programs, projects and activities provided in DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 in the appropriation, allocation and disbursement of Special Education Fund. Also, refrain from charging expenses that are not within the bounds of laws and regulations on SEF so that the same would be utilized exclusively for the purpose/s it was created.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly comply with the provision stated above. Collections for the day should be deposited intact with the authorized depository bank. The balance of collections not deposited during the day due to cut-off should be deposited in the next working day to avoid risks from using the collection for paying obligations and exposing the funds to risk from loss or theft.

We recommended that the management allocate funds from the Special Education Fund for the construction, repair and maintenance of public schools and to adhere with RA7171 for the utilization of tobacco funds.

We recommended that management through the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the above cited provision. Submit on time all the necessary reports together with their complete supporting documents in order not to delay the post audit of transactions and preparation of other reports. It is the direct responsibility of the agency head to install, implement, and monitor sound internal control.

We recommended that management, thru the Bids and Awards Committee adhere with A.O No. 17 to procure common-use supplies from the PS-DBM and ensure strict compliance with the conditions provided for under Section 52.1b of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A 9184 for the procurement of ordinary or regular supplies not available in PS-DBM.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), through its Secretariat post all the Procurement Opportunities in the (PhilGEPS) website, the website of the Procuring Entity concerned, if available, and at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in the premises of the Procuring Entity for a period of at least three (3) calendar days and posting of NOA and Contract/PO within ten (10) days from their issuance as provided in IV-E and L of Annex “H” of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A No. 9184 to promote transparency and efficiency in the procurement process and extend equal opportunity to all the eligible and qualified suppliers.

We recommended that management ensure strict observance of the provisions of Sections 7.2 and 54.1 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184 in the implementation of infrastructure projects. Procurement should be in accordance with law, rules and regulations, with a view of ensuring efficiency, economy and effectiveness in its operations. Public funds shall not be applied to any public use other than for which such fund or property were appropriated by law or ordinance.

We recommended that management use the appropriate fund for the construction, repair and maintenance of school buildings and other facilities for public elementary and secondary schools which is the Special Education Fund (SEF) and to utilize the 20% Development Fund for the purposes specified under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated February 22, 2018.

We recommended that management conduct the necessary field surveys and investigations in the detailed engineering and carefully study/evaluate the site condition in the preparation of accurate design plans, technical specifications, quantity and cost estimates and program of work for infrastructure projects to minimize any work suspensions that may cause delay in the completion of the projects.

We recommended that management, based on the foregoing provisions that additional compensation such as commissions, honoraria, fringe benefits, benefits in excess of the allowable statutory amount of P82,000.00 are not exempt from the mandatory Withholding of Income Tax on Compensation and be remitted on or before the 10th day of the following month in which the withholding was made except for tax returns covering transactions in December which shall be filed on or before January 15 of the succeeding year.
