(BAC) to comply with the regulation on the submission of contracts with complete
supporting documents as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14,
2012.

We have recommended that Management review the hiring procedure and
personnel management of Job Orders and set a standard policy and guidelines
relative thereto in accordance with the regulations set forth in Section 76 and 77 of
RA 7160 and the CSC Resolution No. 020790. Also, we recommend that
Management enjoin the concern officials to submit copies of Job Order Contract or
Contract of Service to the Auditor within five (5) days from its issuance as required
in COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We have recommended that the LCE enjoin the concerned officials to ensure
that the Annual Budget details the specific priority projects identified by the Local
Development Council under the 20% Development Fund. Management should also
require that projects funded out the 20% EDF be implemented as planned or
scheduled.

We have recommended that Management adhere with the provisions of
DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 dated April 13, 2011 on the
use of 20% Economic Development Fund. The Local Development Council should
prioritize and incorporate in the Annual Investment Plan of the LGU those projects
that are aligned with the stated regulation.

We have recommended that Management ensure that any expenditure out of
the Special Education Fund be authorized by the Local School Board pursuant to an
Approved SEF Budget as required under Section 99.b of RA 7160.
The Accountant explained that the SEF expenditures were based on an Approved
School Board Budget, yet this document was only submitted on February 10, 2017, in
compliance with our AOM issued for the subject deficiency.
The document submitted and referred by the Accountant as the budget was a
Local School Board Plan with identified PPAs, but such were not specific and the
members of the school board who certified the plan did not affix their signatures
themselves but a representative signed the plan in their behalf (save the approval of the
LCE).
This School Board Plan could not be considered the Approved School Board
Budget but only a plan which support the budget, and which PPAs should have been
specified. A SEF budget uses a prescribed budget forms, like that of the budget for the
general fund, and such should be approved by the Local School Board through a board
resolution by the members of the board themselves and not by representatives.
In order to assure that such a SEF plan and budget exist, these documents should
have been submitted to our Office immediately after its approval.

We have recommended that Management provide an adequate budget for
solid waste management to ensure effectiveness and sustainability of its Solid Waste
Management programs/projects. Barangays should be required to create Barangay
SWM Committee and prepare and implement solid waste management policies.
We have also recommended that partnership and linkages with schools and
private sector be established to assist in the implementation of SWM programs and
to intensify their IEC campaign pursuant to Section 55 of RA 9003.
Also, the following were recommended:
1. Separation of collection for biodegradable and non-biodegradable
waste pursuant to Section 21 of RA 9003.
2. Rehabilitation of the Municipality’s closed dumpsite and its conversion
controlled dumpsite and thereafter into a sanitary landfill.
3. Organization of trainings for recycling activities and composting
technologies and its adoption.
4. Integration of SWM orientation upon renewal/application of Mayor’s
Permit and business licenses as part of its strategies to ensure an
effective solid waste management program in the entire municipality.

We have recommended that the LCE direct the BuB focal person/(s) to fast
track the implementation of PPAs covered by the CY 2015-2016 Bub Fund releases,
in order to (a) accomplish the purpose for which the funds were granted and (b)
deliver immediate and optimum result to target beneficiaries of the BuB Program.
Delay in the implementation of the planned PPAs must be investigated and resolved
in order to avoid unnecessary returns or reversions of much-needed Funds to
grantor-partner agencies.

We have also recommended that the LCE direct the Accountant to submit
necessary liquidation report and supporting document for the utilized BuB funds
totalling P 1,520,500.00.

We have recommended that the Budget Officer control and monitor the issuance of Obligation Request (OBR) for every transaction. Obligation Request (OBR) should be made available prior to the issuance of Invitation to Bid and should be forwarded to the Head of the Procuring Entity before entering into a contract to prove that there is available appropriation and there is authority issued by the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to incur obligations. Likewise, the Budget Officer and Chief Accountant should reconcile records on allotments available for obligation at the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: Obligation request is issued prior to posting of invitation to bid.
observations.reason: Verification of obligation request revealed that some obligation requests were issued after the posting of invitationto bid.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the LGU to establish an effective way of encouraging the constituents to file their new application or renewal of permits and licenses to increase the amount of collection for the period without using the government funds as a guarantee to collect. Government funds should be managed and spent for public purposes. All resources of the government shall be managed, expended or utilized in accordance with law and regulations and safeguarded against loss or wastage through improper disposition.
observations.managementAction: No payment was made for the premiums for Personal Accident Insurance of tricycle drivers/operators.
observations.reason: Verification of transactions revealed that no payment of premiums for Personal Accident Insurance of tricycle drivers/operators was made.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended management to ensure strict observance of the provisions of Section 54.1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184 in the procurement of goods and services. Purchases shouldbe in accordance with law, rules and regulations, with a view of ensuring efficiency, economy and effectiveness in its operations. Avoid non-consolidation of requisitions, issuance of two or more purchase orders and division of payments for single purchase order.
observations.managementAction: Management strictly implement the last year’s recommendation to avoid splitting of contracts.
observations.reason: Verification of CY2016 revealed that no splitting of contracts were made.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer see to it that the name of the payee in the disbursement voucher and in the check are the same and he/she should always require a special power of attorney (SPA) of the company’s representativeor agent who will receive the payment for a certain transaction to ensure payment to the rightful claimant.
observations.managementAction: Payment of newspaper publication was made directly to the supplier.
observations.reason: Verification of disbursement vouchers for the payment of newspaper publication revealed that payment were made directly to the supplier.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipality to allocate for the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (MDRRMF) amounting to not less than five percent (5%) of the estimated revenue from regular sources to fully achieve the objective of mainstreaming disaster risk reduction and lessening the socioeconomic and environmental impacts of disasters at all levels in the local community.
observations.managementAction: Management appropriated funds for Local Disaster Risk and Reduction Management in the amount of P2,687,500.00
observations.reason: Based on the budget submitted, appropriation for the Local Disaster  Risk and Reduction Management Fund is P2,687,500.00 which isnot less than 5% of the estimated revenue from regular sources.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive should create an Inventory Committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU. Their reports will be reconciled with the book balances, and the differences should be properly accounted for. Then, the Property Custodian shall prepare and update the Property Cards, Real Property Cards, Property Ledger and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger. With these, regular reconciliation of accounting and property records could be undertaken. The periodic physical inventory shall strictly be conducted onwards after the initial count in compliance with Sections 102 and 111 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 80-124. The GSO should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU will completely/accurately accounted for, and the unserviceable properties could be properly accounted, disposed and recorded.
observations.reason: Inventory of properties still is not completed records at the General Services Office do not reconcile with that of the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: Require all concerned accountable officers to immediately liquidate all cash advances and that current cash advances for travel should first be settled before new ones be allowed to the same accountable officers in compliance with the provisions of the cited rules in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.reason: Some employees still has unlquidated balances but are not compelled by the LCE to settle the same.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the concerned officials to comply with the documentary requirements on gasoline and fuel consumption provided in COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 for control purposes.

We recommend that the Accountant be required to submit immediately to validate the authenticity of the procured items. In addition, the Report on Drugs and Medicines Issued for all issued drugs and medicines should be prepared and submitted to the Accountant to serve as basis in recording the used/ expense portion in the books. Henceforth, pay only the transactions with complete supporting documents.

We recommend that Management stop immediately the practice of equating the government share of HDMF to their personal contributions if it exceeds the allowable amount of P 100.00 in a month. Management should make representation with the HDMF officials to refund over-remittance of PAG-IBIG contributions for the calendar year 2015 and previous years or consider the same as advance payment of premiums/contributions.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enjoined the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to submit immediately the unsubmitted Disbursement Vouchers for calendar year 2015. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should submit the monthly reports together with the all vouchers, payrolls and official receipts in accordance with the provisions of Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160 and COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.reason: Disbursement Vouchers totalling P 10,747,987.56 still remain unsubmitted as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a.) Strictly comply with the relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 and post signboards that are meant to uphold good local governance through transparency and accountability, at the least cost or most effective means; b.) Require the Municipal Engineer to inform our Office within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the project that the appropriate projects signboards and/or public notices are already posted at the project site, for proper validation by this Office; and c.) Require the Municipal Engineer to submit quarterly Status Report on implemented government projects.
observations.reason: There were no information or notice/s provided by the Engineer that appropriate signboards were already posted on the project site for projects implemented during the year within ten (10) days as mandated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly follow the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 7, 2012 on the use of Local Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF). The LDRRM Council have to ensure that programs and activities under the 70% of the LDRRMF will directly address disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness issues in the locality and that the same be fully implemented during the year.
observations.reason: LDRRM Fund was still charged with inappropriate expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the General Services Officer (GSO) should issue Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE) to all concerned employees and ARE. The GSO should also renew ARE every two years to determine the condition and existence of these properties.
observations.reason: Some properties issued to employees still do not have the corresponding ARE.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Special Education Fund (SEF) be utilized for the purposes specified under Republic Act No. 5447, Section 272 of the Local Government Code (RA 7160) and Joint Circular No. 01-B of the DECS, DBM and DILG dated June 25, 2001 so that the objectives of the Fund will be attained.
observations.reason: There were still charges of travelling expenses to the Appropriation for SEF.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the LGU officials concerned to strictly comply with the aforecited provisions of the law and regulations so that the required financial reports in relation to Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund for CY 2014 will be submitted by the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant.
observations.reason: Submission of Monthly Report on the Receipts and Utilization of LDRRMF, vouchers and other documents were already complied, except for the submission of separate financial statements for LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: The municipality should adhere to the mandate of R.A. No. 658 for them to be able to avail of financial consideration should natural or man-made calamity arises whether natural or man-made.
